steps: Go to https://gitlab.com and sign in with your GitLab account
Click your avatar in the top-right corner and select 'Edit profile'
Enter a name for your application
Enter a redirect URI (e.g. https://yourapp.com/auth/callback or http://localhost:3000/callback for local dev)
Select the required scopes (at minimum: 'api' for full access, or 'read_api' for read-only)
Copy the Application ID (Client ID) and the Secret (Client Secret) — the secret is shown only once

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Secret (Client Secret)
credential_fields.description: Your GitLab OAuth application secret — shown only at creation

notes: Free GitLab.com accounts can create OAuth applications
Client secret is shown only once at creation; store it securely
For personal scripts, GitLab also supports Personal Access Tokens at https://gitlab.com/-/user_settings/personal_access_tokens (simpler than OAuth for single-user use)
Required scope for full API access: 'api'

test_endpoint.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user
